allies.rationale_text: Psychological-safety supports self-determination-theory by making relatedness and candid learning feel possible inside the group.
Growth-mindset strengthens self-determination-theory by reinforcing competence as something that can be built rather than fixed.
allies.source_quote: Competence: The need to feel effective and capable

allies.rationale_text: Emotional-intelligence helps self-determination-theory read the human conditions that are supporting or suppressing motivation.
allies.source_quote: Relatedness: The need to experience belonging and connection with others

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias can make leaders keep a controlling system while telling themselves people just need more effort.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Self-determination-theory conflicts with incentives when extrinsic rewards crowd out the intrinsic motivation that SDT identifies as the driver of sustained engagement.
structured_tensions.source_quote: self-determination-theory conflicts with incentives (incentives) when extrinsic rewards crowd out the intrinsic motivation that SDT identifies as the driver of sustained engagement.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Self-determination-theory conflicts with specialization when autonomy needs conflict with the organizational constraints that specialization imposes on role scope.
structured_tensions.source_quote: self-determination-theory conflicts with specialization (specialization) when autonomy needs conflict with the organizational constraints that specialization imposes on role scope.
